hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — Q, A. You kicked off the action with a raise, and your opponent decided to call and see a flop.
Flop — 6, Jack, 9. You decided to bet, trying to take control of the pot, and your opponent chose to call again, still in the hunt.
Turn — 3. You bet once more, putting more pressure on your opponent, who opted to call for the second time, remaining patient.
River — Ace. The board paired one of your opponent's hole cards, and they suddenly led out with a confident bet, looking to capitalize on their improved hand.

wrongReasons: Q 9 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than Q 9 would have.
J 9 makes a two pair here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
K 2 has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 4 A

Your opponent called two streets with no apparent hand, then the **Ace** on the river paired one of their hole cards, giving them **One Pair**. The sudden **bet** on the river, after calling twice, indicates they finally hit something and went for value, making **4 A** the most likely hand.
